# CleverTap Geofencing Gallery — Field Guide 08

> An interactive reference of 120 location-based geofencing campaign examples for CleverTap, spanning 15 industries. Each example pairs a real-world scenario with the channel it runs on, a device mockup of the customer-facing message, the SDK event payload, and a copy-paste setup sheet. Built by SJ Neo as part of the CleverTap Field Guide series.

## What this is

This is a single-page gallery and developer guide for marketers and engineers using CleverTap's geofencing capability. It answers "what location-triggered campaign should I run, on which channel, and how do I set it up?"

Geofencing raises Geofence Cluster Entered and Exited events automatically from the mobile SDK when a device crosses a virtual boundary around a real location. Campaigns respond in real time or as scheduled follow-ups.

## Key facts about CleverTap geofencing

- A cluster holds up to 100 geofences; an app can have up to 100 clusters.
- Fences are circles or polygons. Practical positioning accuracy is 20 to 50 metres, so fences should be sized at 100 metres or more.
- Events are raised only by the SDK (Geofence Cluster Entered / Exited). The app requests location permission; the SDK never asks for it.
- Each cluster carries its own tracking schedule and Do Not Disturb hours.
- The event payload includes: id, gcId, gcName, lat, lng, r (radius), triggered_lat, triggered_lng.
- There is no native dwell event. Dwell is built as a Journey: Entered starts a timer, Exited cancels it, surviving the wait counts as dwell.
- Geofencing is an Advanced Plan capability.
- A geofence campaign can respond on any channel: push, in-app, SMS, WhatsApp, email, or webhook.

## Coverage

120 examples across 15 verticals: e-commerce, fintech, food and beverage, travel, media and entertainment, telco, insurance, mobility and ride-hailing, gaming and esports, health and fitness, education, property and proptech, logistics, betting and gaming, and jobs and recruitment.

Four trigger mechanics: entry, exit, dwell, and follow-up.
Six delivery channels: push, in-app, SMS, WhatsApp, email, webhook.

## Sections

- Use-Case Gallery: 120 filterable examples, each opening to device mockups, event payload, cluster and campaign config, and a setup sheet.
- Developer Setup Docs: SDK integration for Android and iOS, permission-flow design, cluster creation, campaign qualification, and payload reference.

## Notes

Mechanics are verified against docs.clevertap.com and developer.clevertap.com. Sample coordinates, brand names, and figures in the mockups are illustrative, not real campaign data.
